# Vendored ReDoc

- **Library:** ReDoc 2.5.1 (standalone bundle)
- **Upstream:** https://github.com/Redocly/redoc
- **Licence:** MIT. Third-party notices for everything bundled into it are in
  `redoc.standalone.js.LICENSE.txt`, kept alongside as upstream ships it.
- **Source of this copy:** `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/redoc@2.5.1/bundles/redoc.standalone.js`
- **SHA-256:** `LY0T6UtaIQEcxvgmDYfKR10Pby+u0W3RLnOU36O7Bbg=`
  (byte-identical to `https://cdn.redocly.com/redoc/v2.5.1/bundles/redoc.standalone.js`,
  verified)

`agents-api.html` and `responder-api.html` used to load this bundle from a CDN,
which made every visitor's browser contact a third party and disclose its IP
address and User-Agent before the page rendered. It is served from this
repository instead, so the API documentation contacts no origin but its own.

## Why 2.5.1 and not the newest release

`responder-api.html` was already pinned to `v2.5.1`, and it calls
`Redoc.hydrate()` against a pre-rendered state embedded in the page. That state
is produced by a specific ReDoc version, so the bundle has to match it: vendoring
2.5.3 (the current npm `latest`) would have been a version upgrade wearing the
costume of a privacy fix, and a mismatch shows up as a page that renders
nothing. This copy is the exact file that page has been loading.

`agents-api.html` had no such constraint, because it was loading nothing at all
— see below — so it shares this one bundle rather than adding a second megabyte
to the repository.

## agents-api.html was broken

It pointed at `cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/redoc@next/bundles/redoc.standalone.js`,
which **returns HTTP 404**: the `next` tag moved to 3.0.0-rc.0, and ReDoc 3 no
longer publishes `bundles/redoc.standalone.js`. The page had been loading a
71-byte plain-text error as if it were JavaScript, so the `<redoc>` element was
never upgraded and the documentation never appeared. Serving the bundle from
here fixes that as a side effect.

## Updating

Download the same URL for the new version, check the hash, replace both this
file and `redoc.standalone.js.LICENSE.txt`, and update the version and hash
above. Do not point either page back at a moving tag: `next` is what broke one
page, and `latest` would have silently re-pinned the other to whatever upstream
published that day.

If `responder-api.html` stops rendering after an update, its pre-rendered
`__redoc_state` was generated by a different ReDoc version and has to be
regenerated with the matching `redoc-cli`.
